page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, establishing a 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. A primary hardware feature is the bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD local storage, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks show average capabilities for standard compute tasks, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance falling into the middle 50% tier. However, memory bandwidth for small blocks is weak, while large block read and write operations maintain average speeds. Complex processing tasks show limitations, with Geekbench scores, Passmark CPU scores, and software compilation benchmarks ranking in the weak to poor categories. Cryptographic operations benefit from hardware-accelerated AES support, but the lack of AVX or AMX extensions limits vector and matrix math performance, as reflected in poor LLM inference speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the im4gn.xlarge offers high local storage density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for storage-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU performance. The combination of dedicated ARM64 cores and a large NVMe SSD makes this instance suitable for database operations, caching layers, and storage-intensive applications where local read/write speeds are prioritized over raw computational throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized server utilizing the ARM64 architecture and powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of memory. A defining characteristic of this instance is its bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD local storage, supported by a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average stress-ng CPU performance, though memory bandwidth for small blocks and complex computational tasks like software compilation and LLM inference rank in the weak to poor categories. Given its high local storage capacity relative to its compute profile, the im4gn.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy applications. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and storage-intensive workloads that prioritize high-capacity local NVMe storage over high-performance compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD for storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server with 4 dedicated cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 1875 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and a high-capacity bundled NVMe SSD for storage-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
Includes 16.0 GB of system memory and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Equipped with a high-capacity 1875 GB NVMe SSD local storage drive
Delivers average CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Optimized for database hosting, caching, and storage-heavy workloads
